phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest toilets are
phrases.text_local: Où sont les toilettes les plus proches ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo son lay twah-LET lay plew PROHSH
phrases.cultural_note: Public toilets can be hard to find; cafés usually allow customers to use theirs.

responses.english: There are public toilets at the bottom of the square.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah day twah-LET pub-LEEK ahn bah duh lah PLASS
responses.french: Il y a des toilettes publiques en bas de la place.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find the best street food
phrases.text_local: Où trouver le meilleur street food ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo troo-VAY leh may-YUHR street FOOD
phrases.cultural_note: The area has a young, creative vibe with food trucks, falafel shops, and trendy cafés. Try the falafel at 'Mama' or 'Sultan'.

responses.english: There are several stands on this street. Try over there.
responses.phonetic: say par lah, zhoost oh boo duh lah roo
responses.french: Il y a plusieurs stands dans cette rue. Essayez par là.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un marché le dimanche ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL uhn mar-SHAY luh dee-MANSH
phrases.cultural_note: There's a brocante (flea market) on Sundays. It's a great place to find vintage treasures and antiques.

responses.english: Yes, on Sunday mornings, there's a flea market.
responses.french: Oui, le dimanche matin, il y a une brocante.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there is an ATM nearby
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un distributeur de billets par ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL uhn dee-stree-boo-TUHR duh bee-YAY par ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: ATMs (distributeurs) are common in France. Look for signs saying 'DAB' or the bank logos.

responses.english: Yes, there's one at the corner of the street.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ee ahn ah uhn oh KWAN duh lah ROO
responses.french: Oui, il y en a un au coin de la rue.
